message:
feat(frankenpandas): re-export 23 fp_types::nan* primitives at top level + prelude (br-q554z / fd90.126)

fd90.126: NanOps code block at README lines 784-810 calls 23 bare nan*
functions (nansum, nanmean, nancount, nanmin, nanmax, nanmedian,
nanvar, nanstd, nansem, nanprod, nanptp, nanskew, nankurt, nanquantile,
nanargmax, nanargmin, nannunique, nanany, nanall, nancumsum, nancumprod,
nancummax, nancummin) but none were re-exported from frankenpandas.
A user copy-pasting hits 23 E0425 'cannot find function' errors.

The README block has no 'use fp_types::*' or qualification, reading as
if the prelude exposes these. fd90.121 + fd90.125 set the precedent
that the prelude should match the documented surface.

Fix:
1. Re-export all 23 nan* functions at the top-level frankenpandas
   crate (alongside cast_scalar/common_dtype/etc).
2. Add them to the prelude.

Verified:
- cargo build -p frankenpandas — compiles cleanly
- cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib — all 5 prelude tests pass

Pure re-export expansion. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,6 +19,13 @@
 pub use fp_types::{DType, NullKind, Scalar};
 pub use fp_types::{cast_scalar, common_dtype, infer_dtype, isna, isnull, notna, notnull};
 
+// NanOps: null-skipping aggregation primitives (matches README's NanOps section).
+pub use fp_types::{
+    nanall, nanany, nanargmax, nanargmin, nancount, nancummax, nancummin, nancumprod, nancumsum,
+    nankurt, nanmax, nanmean, nanmedian, nanmin, nannunique, nanprod, nanptp, nanquantile, nansem,
+    nanskew, nanstd, nansum, nanvar,
+};
+
 pub use fp_columnar::{ArithmeticOp, Column, ColumnError, ComparisonOp, ValidityMask};
 
 pub use fp_index::{
@@ -216,6 +223,30 @@ pub mod prelude {
         to_datetime,
         to_numeric,
         to_timedelta,

+        nanall,

+        nancummin,

         // IO — writers (in-memory + path + sql, matching README documented surface)
         write_csv,
         write_csv_string,
